title: viola canina var. montana
intro: Viola canina var. montana, also known as the mountain pimpernel or mountain dog violet, is a flowering plant species in the violet family that belongs to the Viola canina species. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it useful for wound healing and skin conditions. The health benefits of Viola canina var. montana include its ability to reduce inflammation and promote wound healing, as well as its potential to alleviate symptoms of anxiety and depression. Herbal preparations of the plant include infusions, tinctures, and salves, which can be made by steeping the leaves and flowers in hot water or by mixing the plant material with a solvent like ethanol.
